title: Streaming Analysis, Chunk Boundaries, and Resource Budgets · Code Recall
summary: A delayed maintenance review reconstructs streaming analysis, chunk boundaries, and resource budgets from repository state, input bytes, decoded text, tokens, counters, ranking keys, report records, stdout, stderr, exit status, tests, package artifact, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bounded streaming analyzer after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Process lines or bounded chunks incrementally and keep only state required by the declared report.
Carry a bounded token fragment across chunk boundaries and prove concatenated-chunk equivalence.
Use an incremental decoder or text stream that preserves multibyte sequences and explicit error policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare batch and streaming results across adversarial split points, malformed data, and final fragments.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Batch Stream Equivalence, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Batch Stream Equivalence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Batch Stream Equivalence, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review Bookbot team handles case 5690 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove streaming and batch equivalence under token-fragment and peak-memory bounds; keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare batch and streaming results across adversarial split points, malformed data, and final fragments.

For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Batch Stream Equivalence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: One ordinary file is sufficient evidence that streaming matches batch analysis.
